pi_items.source: Verified by checking other task.json files in the same environment directory which all use the same env_id
Version field in task.json

README.md section states 'Difficulty: 🟡 Medium'. Task requires multiple skills (duplicate detection, gap identification, dietary validation, formula creation) justifying medium difficulty classification
Setup_task.sh creates CSV with dates starting from 2025-03-01. Verified via date parsing that March 1, 2025 is Saturday
Setup_task.sh creates CSV with dates ending at 2025-03-14. Verified via date parsing that March 14, 2025 is Friday
Calculated (2025-03-14 minus 2025-03-01 plus 1 = 14 days). All days from March 1-14 are consecutive with no gaps

pi_items.source: CSV data in setup_task.sh: entries jump from 2025-03-06 (line 21) to 2025-03-08 (line 22), skipping March 7. Total of 12 covered dates out of required 14

pi_items.verified_value: 14 meal entries in CSV (including 2 duplicates for single dates)
pi_items.source: CSV data in setup_task.sh has 14 data rows (excluding header): lines 15-28. This covers 12 unique dates with 2 duplicate dates

pi_items.verified_value: Task requires all 14 days (March 1-14, 2025) to have exactly one vegetarian meal each
pi_items.source: Task description states 'She needs 14 consecutive days (March 1-14, 2025) of vegetarian dinners' and natural_language prompt specifies requirement. Initial data has 2 meat violations (March 5, 9) that violate this requirement

Setup_task.sh creates data inline using bash heredoc (cat > file << 'CSVEOF'). No external data downloads, APIs, or public sources referenced. Data is hardcoded as test fixture with intentional conflicts
init.timeout_sec in task.json equals 180 seconds (3 minutes)
init.max_steps in task.json equals 15
init.reward_type in task.json equals 'sparse'

pi_items.verified_value: 6 verification criteria (Complete Coverage, Dietary Compliance, No Date Conflicts, Conflict Analysis, Gap Resolution, Summary Present). Pass threshold is 70% (4 out of 6 criteria)
pi_items.source: README.md states 6 verification criteria and 'Pass Threshold: 70% (requires at least 4 out of 6 criteria)'. Verifier.py implements identical logic at lines 292-366

pi_items.verified_value: Date, Volunteer_Name, Dish_Planned, Contains_Meat, Contact_Info (5 columns)
pi_items.source: CSV header in setup_task.sh line 14

pi_items.verified_value: Date column: index 0, Volunteer_Name: index 1, Dish_Planned: index 2, Contains_Meat: index 3, Contact_Info: index 4
pi_items.source: Verifier.py references date_column_idx=0 (line 113) and meat_column_idx=3 (line 131). CSV structure confirms this mapping

privileged_info_summary: The meal train organizer task uses synthetic data created inline in setup_task.sh. The dataset contains 14 CSV entries covering dates March 1-14, 2025 (14 consecutive days). The initial data intentionally includes: 2 duplicate date assignments (March 3 and March 11 each have 2 volunteers), 2 coverage gaps (March 7 and March 10 with no entries), and 2 dietary violations (March 5: Meatloaf and March 9: BBQ Chicken both marked Contains_Meat=Yes). The task requires completing the 14-day coverage and ensuring all meals are vegetarian. Verification requires passing 4 of 6 criteria: complete coverage, dietary compliance, no date conflicts, conflict analysis evidence, gap resolution, and summary statistics. Timeout is 180 seconds with a maximum of 15 steps.
